overall_assessment.one_sentence_verdict: Strong architecture-first AI platform evidence with production RAG, evaluation harnesses, guardrails, and player-coach mentoring aligns well with this senior IC category, though headline framing and agent orchestration depth need refinement.
overall_assessment.uncertainty_note: Representation is proxy-derived; headline currently uses 'Technical Architect' rather than Staff/Principal AI Engineer framing that matches 96% of target roles.
citations: Platform design appears in 24/25 roles (96%); candidate has Commander-4 platform lead evidence at data/master-cv/roles/01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28
RAG 9/25 (36%), evaluation_quality 5/25 (20%), guardrails_governance 7/25 (28%) — all supported by Commander-4 curated evidence

weighted_score_explanation: Evidence coverage (8.5 × 0.30 = 2.55): Platform architecture, RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and distributed systems all strongly supported by Commander-4 and legacy modernization evidence.
Curation completeness (7.5 × 0.15 = 1.125): Core AI platform work is curated; some upstream STAR records and Daypaio evidence remain pending promotion.

Representation quality (7.0 × 0.25 = 1.75): AI platform and reliability metrics are surfaced, but headline uses 'Technical Architect' instead of Staff/Principal AI Engineer title family that matches 96% of roles.

AI architecture fit (8.0 × 0.15 = 1.20): Strong RAG, hybrid search, evaluation harness, and guardrails evidence; agent orchestration is present via MCP tools but not as deeply articulated as multi-agent patterns.
Leadership scope fit (8.0 × 0.10 = 0.80): Player-coach mentoring of 10+ engineers with 3 promotions fits category scope; hiring involvement supports but does not over-claim.
Impact proof strength (8.5 × 0.05 = 0.425): Quantified metrics include 75% incident reduction, 3 years zero downtime, 2,000 users, 42 plugins, and 25% velocity improvement.
citations: data/master-cv/roles/01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28 — Commander-4 platform lead with hybrid search, LLM-as-judge, semantic caching, evaluation harness

strongest_supported_signals.signal: AI platform architecture with production RAG and hybrid retrieval
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Platform design appears in 24/25 roles (96%) and RAG in 9/25 (36%); architecture is the top valued evidence type in deep-analysis samples.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: BM25 + RRF hybrid search with LLM-as-judge reranking via Claude Sonnet serving 2,000 users across 42 plugins

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Evaluation harness with retrieval quality metrics
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Evaluation_quality appears in 5/25 (20%) and is a differentiator; this category values AI quality and production behavior over generic prompt claims.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: MRR@k and NDCG@k (exponential gain) scoring functions with 14 unit tests for retrieval quality evaluation

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Guardrails and governed structured outputs for enterprise AI
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Guardrails_governance appears in 7/25 (28%); shows practical controls and safe production behavior.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Zod schema validation, composable guardrail profiles across 42 plugins, per-silo guardrail injection via LiteLLM proxy with access-control-aware behavior

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Legacy platform modernization with reliability outcomes
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Reliability is cited in 18 deep-analysis samples; success metrics cluster around reliability (4), performance (3), scalability (2).
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Transformed legacy JS AdTech platform to TypeScript microservices on AWS; achieved 75% incident reduction and 3 years zero downtime

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Player-coach mentoring with quantified team development
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Player_coach 11/25 (44%) and mentoring 12/25 (48%); shows technical leadership without overstating formal management.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Mentored 10+ senior engineers on architectural patterns, event-driven design, and cloud best practices; promoted 3 engineers to lead positions

strongest_supported_signals.signal: MCP tool design and external system integrations
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Agent orchestration appears in 12/25 (48%); MCP tool design shows production AI integration capability.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: 5 MCP server tools for external system integrations within Commander-4 enterprise AI workflow platform

strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Distributed systems appears in 7/25 (28%); backend foundations matter under the AI layer.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: EventBridge choreography, autonomous event-driven TypeScript microservices, DDD with bounded contexts

gap_analysis.signal: Staff/Principal AI Engineer title framing in headline
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Title families cluster around principal_engineer 13/25 (52%) and staff_engineer 11/25 (44%); current headline uses 'Technical Architect' which may not trigger ATS or recruiter pattern matching for this category.
gap_analysis.current_state: Headline reads 'Technical Architect / AI Platform Architect' per data/master-cv/role_metadata.json:4
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Evidence supports Staff AI Engineer positioning given platform lead role and 11 years experience.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Update headline to 'Staff AI Engineer | AI Platform Architecture, Distributed Systems, Production GenAI' per blueprint safe title families.
gap_analysis.citation: Blueprint recommends Staff/Principal framing first because title families cluster around principal_engineer 13/25 (52%) and staff_engineer 11/25 (44%)

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Agents_orchestration appears in 12/25 (48%); showing reusable agent workflow systems and orchestration logic is a differentiator.
gap_analysis.current_state: MCP tools (5) are curated but broader agent orchestration patterns are not emphasized in current representation.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: MCP integration is valid agent orchestration evidence; can be positioned as agent-enabling infrastructure.

gap_analysis.citation: Category values shipped agent workflows and reusable tool integrations; agents_orchestration 12/25 (48%)

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Observability appears in 6/25 (24%); scale proof strengthens reliability narrative valued by this category.
gap_analysis.current_state: Observability pipeline with billions of events daily is curated at data/master-cv/roles/01_seven_one_entertainment.md:103 but not prominently surfaced in role priorities.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Evidence is strong and can be elevated in experience bullets for this category.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Elevate observability pipeline achievement to top-4 experience bullets for Staff AI Engineer applications; include 'billions of events' scale marker.
gap_analysis.citation: Valued evidence types include reliability 18 samples; hard skill observability 6/25 (24%)

gap_analysis.signal: Semantic caching latency and cost reduction metrics
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Production reliability and cost efficiency matter for platform delivery; quantified caching impact proves production judgment.
gap_analysis.current_state: Two-tier semantic caching with L1 ~2ms and L2 ~200ms is curated but cost/latency reduction percentages are not quantified.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Cache architecture is proven; can claim latency tiers without fabricating reduction percentages.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Surface semantic caching latency tiers explicitly in experience bullets; add cost reduction claim only if Lantern project 40% reduction applies to Commander-4.
gap_analysis.citation: L1 exact-match ~2ms, L2 semantic similarity ~200ms via Redis TTL and S3 Vectors cosine search

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Collaboration appears in 23/25 (92%); this is the most consistent soft skill signal in the category.
gap_analysis.current_state: Stakeholder alignment is mentioned in Technical Vision achievement but collaboration is not emphasized in summary or headline.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Evidence supports cross-functional collaboration claims across AdTech and AI platform work.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Add 'cross-functional collaboration' to summary positioning; emphasize product owner and enterprise architect alignment in experience bullets.
gap_analysis.citation: Collaboration 23/25 (92%) is highest soft skill; blueprint recommends showing how you aligned product, platform, security, and engineering stakeholders

gap_analysis.signal: Go language depth for backend systems
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Go appears in 4/25 (16%) of roles; some Staff AI Engineer roles expect Go expertise.
gap_analysis.current_state: No curated evidence of Go development; primary languages are TypeScript and Python.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Do not claim Go expertise; position TypeScript/Python as primary backend languages with Node.js and Python-based ML integration.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Do not add Go claims; focus on TypeScript distributed systems and Python AI layer evidence.
gap_analysis.citation: Blueprint unsafe claims include 'Deep production backend delivery in Go unless the CV clearly proves Go depth'

gap_analysis.signal: Direct SLO/SLI ownership and incident response programs
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Some roles expect direct SLO/SLI ownership; blueprint flags this as an unsafe claim without explicit evidence.
gap_analysis.current_state: MTTR reduction and incident management culture are mentioned but formal SLO/SLI ownership is not documented.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Can claim incident reduction outcomes and monitoring/alerting implementation; should not claim SLO program ownership.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Position as reliability-focused architect with incident reduction outcomes; avoid claiming SLO/SLI program ownership unless Lantern project SLO/SLI Design skill is promoted to Commander-4.
gap_analysis.citation: Blueprint unsafe claims include 'Ownership of SLOs, incident response, or reliability programs unless the CV shows direct operational scope'

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Multi_cloud appears in 8 deep-analysis samples; some roles expect cross-cloud platform work.
gap_analysis.current_state: Evidence shows AWS-centric delivery (Lambda, ECS, EventBridge, S3, CloudFront); Lantern mentions OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure endpoints but not full multi-cloud infrastructure.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Can claim AWS platform delivery and multi-provider LLM integration; should not claim full multi-cloud infrastructure implementation.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Position as AWS-experienced with multi-provider LLM gateway integration; avoid claiming GCP/Azure infrastructure deployment.
gap_analysis.citation: Blueprint warns against 'Full multi-cloud implementation across Bedrock and Vertex AI if the work was only on one cloud or one provider'

profile_safe: Architected and shipped enterprise AI workflow platform serving 2,000 users with 42 workflow plugins
Built production RAG systems with hybrid retrieval (BM25 + RRF fusion) and LLM-as-judge reranking
Designed evaluation harnesses with MRR@k and NDCG@k retrieval quality metrics
Implemented governed structured outputs and per-silo guardrails for multi-tenant AI safety
Transformed legacy platforms into event-driven TypeScript microservices achieving 75% incident reduction
Led observability pipeline channeling billions of events daily with real-time dashboards
Player-coach technical leader mentoring 10+ engineers and promoting 3 to lead positions
experience_safe: Platform lead for Commander-4 (Joyia) enterprise AI workflow platform at ProSiebenSat.1
BM25 scoring from scratch for S3 Vectors with RRF fusion (k=60)
LLM-as-judge reranking via Claude Sonnet through LiteLLM gateway with parallel execution
Two-tier semantic caching with L1 exact-match (~2ms) and L2 semantic similarity (~200ms)
5 MCP server tools for external system integrations
Zod schema validation for type-safe LLM outputs across 42 workflow plugins
3 years zero downtime on revenue-critical AdTech platform
DDD bounded contexts reducing developer onboarding from 6 to 3 months
leadership_safe: Mentored 10+ senior engineers on architectural patterns, event-driven design, and cloud best practices
Established architectural north star and technical vision with stakeholder alignment
Led hiring process including selection criteria, interview strategies, and technical assessments
Cross-functional collaboration with product owners, enterprise architects, and stakeholders
unsafe_or_too_weak: Go language expertise — no curated evidence of Go development
SLO/SLI program ownership — incident reduction shown but not formal SLO programs
Direct MCP/OpenAI/Anthropic platform engineering — integration only, not platform development
Multi-cloud infrastructure deployment — AWS-focused with multi-provider LLM routing only
Formal people management or hiring authority — player-coach scope only
Fine-tuning, custom embedding training, or RLHF — explicitly flagged in do_not_claim
Millions-scale user claims — 2,000 platform users documented, not consumer-scale
citations: data/master-cv/projects/commander4_skills.json:42 — do_not_claim: fine-tuning, RLHF, custom embedding training

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Lead the CV as a Staff/Principal AI Engineer architecture-focused senior IC, not as a generic Technical Architect.
Make RAG, hybrid retrieval, and evaluation harnesses prominent in experience section — these are differentiators for this category.
Show guardrails and governed structured outputs as enterprise AI production safety proof.
Emphasize agent orchestration via MCP tools and workflow plugin system as reusable AI platform capability.
Promote observability pipeline with billions-scale marker to top experience priorities.
Position mentoring and player-coach influence without inflating to formal people management claims.
Curate upstream Daypaio CQRS/EventStore evidence to strengthen distributed systems backend foundation.
Do not claim Go language depth, SLO program ownership, or multi-cloud infrastructure deployment.
Do not claim fine-tuning, custom embedding training, or RLHF — explicitly flagged in do_not_claim.
Frame non-AI backend/platform history as the foundation for production AI delivery rather than hiding it.
